reforms.description: A reform package is being developed to streamline the administration of research grants by reducing the number of budget lines from 22 to 10 and centralizing policy powers in the Research and Development Council. Additionally, the Czech Republic plans to tighten responsibilities and streamline financial arrangements related to innovation policy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining grant administration is expected to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of public funding for research, thereby supporting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform package is currently being developed and has been officially announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in the administration of research grants.
reforms.source_quote: A reform package is being developed that includes a reduction in the number of budget lines (from 22 to 10), further centralisation of policy and executive powers in the Research and Development Council, as well as overhaul of the administration of research grants.

reforms.description: Further tax deductions on R&D expenditures are being considered to enhance private-sector investment in research and development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing tax deductions for R&D is likely to incentivize private investment in research, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is currently under consideration and has not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the reform is not as comprehensive as structural changes in grant administration.
reforms.source_quote: Further tax deductions on R&D expenditures are also being considered.

reforms.description: The government plans to allocate a larger share of R&D support on a project basis rather than an institutional basis, aiming to enhance the impact of public funding on innovation outcomes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Shifting to project-based funding is expected to improve the effectiveness of public R&D investments, thereby supporting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is currently planned and has not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in funding strategy but is not as comprehensive as a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Follow-through with plans to allocate a larger share of R&D support on a project, rather than institutional basis.

reforms.description: The government intends to more strongly link earnings to performance in public-sector research institutions, aiming to improve accountability and outcomes in R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Linking earnings to performance is expected to enhance accountability and drive better outcomes in public R&D, supporting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is currently planned and has not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant but does not represent a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: More strongly link earnings to performance in public-sector research institutions.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic has adjusted its investment incentives by reducing the length of corporate tax relief from 10 to 5 years and lowering the ceiling for applying to investment grants, which aims to attract more domestic companies and improve the quality of manufacturing investments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Adjusting investment incentives is expected to attract more domestic investment, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been successfully implemented and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in investment incentives.
reforms.source_quote: The length of corporate tax relief, which has been reduced recently from 10 to 5 years (effective from mid-2007)...

reforms.description: CzechTrade, the national trade promotion agency, provides direct subsidies and support services to SMEs to enhance their international competitiveness, including training programs and participation in trade fairs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing subsidies and support services to SMEs is expected to enhance their competitiveness and foster innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The program is currently in effect and providing support to SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This program is significant for supporting SMEs but does not represent a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: Public support for SMEs comes through a wide range of financial and non-financial schemes, and some of these specifically aim to help businesses tap into globalisation.
